summary: Story 1.4c is well sourced and testable for the diary creation UI, but it is blocked by invalid executor assignment for a primary Frontend story and by upstream dependency blockers in Stories 1.4a and 1.4b.

template_compliance.notes: Required story sections are present, including Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Verification, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. No obvious template placeholders were found.

executor_assignment.notes: The story is Primary Type Frontend and centers on a Livewire creation UI. The validation task and story template map Design/UI Components work to executor @ux-design-expert with quality_gate @dev. The story currently sets executor @dev and quality_gate @ux-design-expert; @ux-design-expert is not in validate-next-story's allowed quality_gate set.

dependency_sequence.notes: Story 1.4c explicitly requires Stories 1.4a and 1.4b. Story 1.4a is currently NO-GO, and Story 1.4b is therefore also NO-GO due dependency sequence.

goal_context_clarity.notes: The story value, third-slice interpretation, diary-only UI scope, and redirect constraint are explicit.

technical_guidance.notes: The story identifies the tenant-scoped create route, existing middleware/profile gate usage, MemoryRecordEditor Livewire component, service delegation, validation, redirect behavior, translations, forbidden controls, and route/profile regression coverage.

reference_effectiveness.notes: Claims were cross-checked against PRD Story 1.4, UI architecture route hierarchy, route responsibilities, Livewire action pattern, critical coding rules, API rules, source tree guidance, and existing memory route/profile components.

self_containment.notes: A frontend/UI executor has enough context to implement the diary create route and Livewire form once service and schema dependencies exist.

testing_guidance.notes: Focused Livewire tests cover member access, profile gate redirect, non-member denial, unsupported type behavior, required validation, successful creation, highlight persistence through service, forbidden control absence, route inspection, service regression, and Pint formatting.

security_scope.notes: Route, middleware, profile gate, policy/service privacy boundaries, direct URL denial, and absence of public sharing/visibility/media/AI controls are explicit.

coderabbit_integration.notes: The section is populated and relevant, but its Specialized Agent Assignment mirrors the invalid executor/quality gate inversion. It should be updated after the Executor Assignment is corrected.

code_intelligence.notes: isCodeIntelAvailable returned false in this session.

anti_hallucination.notes: No invented product feature was found. The story intentionally excludes Story 1.5 detail/edit routes and unsupported media/period/public-sharing scope.

dev_readiness.notes: Implementation should not start until executor assignment is corrected and Stories 1.4a/1.4b are approved and implemented.

blockers.title: Invalid executor assignment for Frontend/UI story
blockers.evidence: Story 1.4c declares Primary Type: Frontend and scopes work to a Livewire diary creation UI, but Executor Assignment is executor @dev and quality_gate @ux-design-expert.
blockers.required_fix: Either keep Primary Type Frontend and update Executor Assignment to executor @ux-design-expert and quality_gate @dev, or reclassify the story as Code/Features/Logic and use a valid gate such as @architect while listing @ux-design-expert as a supporting reviewer.

blockers.title: Required upstream stories are not implementation-ready
blockers.evidence: Story 1.4c requires Story 1.4a and Story 1.4b. Story 1.4a is NO-GO, and Story 1.4b is NO-GO because 1.4a is blocked.
blockers.required_fix: Resolve and complete Story 1.4a, then revalidate/complete Story 1.4b before starting Story 1.4c.

should_fix.title: Align CodeRabbit agent assignment after executor correction
should_fix.recommendation: If the story remains Frontend, list @ux-design-expert as executor/primary UI agent and @dev as quality gate or required implementation review.

nice_to_have: Jira child story sync remains pending; local story artifacts are the source of truth until PO/DevOps explicitly sync them.
recommended_command: /agent aiox-sm Address PO-BLOCK-1.4C-001 in docs/stories/1.4c.diary-record-creation-ui.story.md after resolving Story 1.4a/1.4b sequence blockers
